news.title:
MOFSL sees FY27 as favorable for Indian equities post FY26 underperformance caused by FII outflows and geopolitical risks.
Top stock picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, ICICI Bank, M&M, Titan, Infosys, and TVS Motors; mid-caps outperform large-caps.
news.summary:

news.title:
JM Financial discusses Indian IT stocks amid AI risks & West Asia crisis, highlighting Infosys, TCS, TechM, Wipro.
Top picks include Infosys, Mphasis; target prices set for TCS at Rs 2,660, HCL Tech at Rs 1,440, and Coforge at Rs 1,300.

news.title:
Indian IT firms, including TCS, Infosys, and HCLTech, face subdued Q4 with ~10% YoY growth driven by weak rupee.
Wars, weak discretionary spending, and AI concerns strain client budgets, making next fiscal's revenue key for investors.
news.summary:
Indian IT firms, including TCS, Infosys, and HCLTech, face subdued Q4 with ~10% YoY growth driven by weak rupee.

news.title:
Sensex gained 185 points to close at 73,319.55; Nifty 50 rebounded to 22,713.10, up 0.15%.
Sumeet Bagadia recommends buying Infosys, Kalyan Jewellers, Supriya Lifescience, MM Forgings, and Vedanta.
news.summary:

news.title:
SEBI's proposal to revive open market buybacks may boost shareholder confidence amid volatility, offering flexibility for companies to deploy surplus cash and support stock prices.
Infosys, GHCL, eClerx, and Bajaj Consumer have cumulatively deployed over ₹20,000 crore in FY25-26 buybacks, signaling confidence in intrinsic value.
news.summary:
SEBI's proposal to revive open market buybacks may boost shareholder confidence amid volatility, offering flexibility for companies to deploy surplus cash and support stock prices.
Infosys, GHCL, eClerx, and Bajaj Consumer have cumulatively deployed over ₹20,000 crore in FY25-26 buybacks, signaling confidence in intrinsic value.

news.title:
Kotak Institutional Equities highlights TCS, Infosys, Tech Mahindra, and Coforge as top IT sector picks despite muted Q4FY26 growth and constrained FY27 visibility.
TCS shows stable margins; Infosys expects 3-5% FY27 growth; Coforge leads Q4FY26 with 19.7% YoY growth; Tech Mahindra reports flat revenue but margin gains.
news.summary:
Kotak Institutional Equities highlights TCS, Infosys, Tech Mahindra, and Coforge as top IT sector picks despite muted Q4FY26 growth and constrained FY27 visibility.

news.title:
Infosys to announce Q4 results and FY27 guidance on April 23, with expected double-digit growth in net profit and sales.
Brokerages forecast FY27 revenue growth of 2-5%, margins at 20-22%, and focus on deal wins, Iran war, and GenAI impact.
news.summary:
Infosys to announce Q4 results and FY27 guidance on April 23, with expected double-digit growth in net profit and sales.
Brokerages forecast FY27 revenue growth of 2-5%, margins at 20-22%, and focus on deal wins, Iran war, and GenAI impact.

news.title:
Dolat Capital sees IT sector valuations corrected; Infosys, LTIMindtree, and mid-tier players like Coforge, Persistent Systems, KPIT excel.
AI-led spending and execution-focused strategies to drive selective growth; mid-tier firms show 17-25% CAGR outperformance.

news.title:
Infosys received tax assessment orders for multiple years, expecting a refund of ₹1,745 crore with interest.
Refund pertains to AYs 2013-14 to 2021-22; financial impact for FY 2025-26 under evaluation.

news.title:
Indian mid-sized IT firms like Persistent, Hexaware, and Firstsource focus on Asia and Africa for growth amid US slowdown.
Persistent diversifies via acquisitions; Infosys, Wipro expand in Australia; TCS grows in India with large contracts.

news.title:
Nifty IT fell 1.5% as fears of Iran's threats to subsea cables heightened risks to India's digital connectivity.
TCS rose, while Infosys, Persistent Systems, OFSS, LTIMindtree, and others declined. Analysts see volatility ahead for IT stocks.
news.summary:

news.title:
Mcap of 7 top firms fell by ₹1.75 trn; Reliance lost ₹89,720.3 cr, HDFC Bank ₹37,248.6 cr.
L&T, Bajaj Finance, Infosys gained; geopolitical tensions drove market volatility, Sensex fell 1.3%.
